title: Narrow client scope from 6 to 3 (Claude/Codex/Cursor)
description: Mechanical removal of Windsurf / Roo Code / Gemini CLI support. ClientKind type narrowed to 'claude'|'codex'|'cursor' — TypeScript surfaces every consumer. 15 files: cli config (json/toml/resolver/writer), shared client-kind, schemas (passthrough preserved for legacy keys), i18n messages, 6 template files, 5 doc updates. Critical: zod schema preserves passthrough() so users with legacy clientPaths don't see hard validation errors — 1.7.1 (TASK-037) surfaces them as deprecation warnings instead. Add clientScope test fixture preventing future reintroduction. SHOULD precede TASK-018 (don't snapshot dying clients).
scope: Layer 2 P0 — first consumer task; mechanical scope reduction across 15 files

files.change: Narrow ClientKind type from union of 6 to 'claude' | 'codex' | 'cursor'
Remove windsurf/rooCode/geminiCLI specific branches; keep claude + cursor JSON writers
Preserve zod passthrough() so legacy clientPaths.{windsurf,rooCode,geminiCLI} don't hard-fail validation
Delete husky templates targeting retired clients (preserve generic husky base)
Audit; remove geminiCLI-specific hooks if any
NEW: test fixture asserting ClientKind has exactly 3 values; failing build if Windsurf/Roo/Gemini reintroduced
Update 5 doc files referencing 6-client matrix → 3-client matrix

criteria: grep -ri 'windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI' packages/*/src returns 0 hits in source code (excluding deprecation warning code in TASK-037 which lives only in 1.7.1 cherry-pick)
User configs with legacy clientPaths still parse (zod passthrough verified)

metadata.sequencing_note: SHOULD precede TASK-018 (Gemini G4: don't snapshot dying clients)
